entry.title: Experience Report: ABAP Code Retreat 2024 – A Beginner's Perspective
entry.content:
ABAP Code Retreat 2024 at Inwerken AG

On September 10th, 2024, the ABAP Code Retreat took place once again in Isernhagen (Hannover). Originally launched in 2016 by @majcon and @ceedee666 this event gathers ABAP developers to code and learn together. The focus of the retreat was on Test Driven Development (TDD), with challenges like "Game of Life" or the "Bowling Kata" used to demonstrate this methodology. The event at Inwerken AG was organized and coordinated by @EnnoWulff , who ensured everything ran smoothly. As beginners, we would like to share our impressions and experiences from this inspiring workshop.

What were our expectations?

Before the event, we had mixed feelings about the ABAP Code Retreat. On one hand, we were excited to expand our knowledge, on the other, we were concerned about being overwhelmed as newcomers. We were curious to see how developers from other companies tackle problems and expected to be challenged, both by the topic and by engaging with more experienced developers.

How did it turn out?

Our initial concerns quickly disappeared once we received a thorough introduction to Test Driven Development. This helped us understand the goal of the workshop and the importance of the methodology. Thanks to the support from both the other participants and the moderator, we never felt left behind and were able to follow along at a comfortable pace.

Pair Programming was one of the biggest challenges, but also one of the most rewarding experiences. Working as a team was essential, and it helped us organize our thought process around solving problems. The alternating roles were particularly beneficial. While one person coded, the other could think through the next steps. This shift in perspective allowed both partners to contribute in different, valuable ways.

It was also fascinating to see how developers from other companies approached and solved similar challenges. And, of course, it was a great opportunity to make new connections that might extend beyond the workshop itself.

What did we learn?

A key takeaway from the retreat was an introduction to Test Driven Development (TDD). This approach is very different from typical development workflows. Instead of jumping straight into coding, TDD starts with the question: "What should my method produce as the final result?". The first step is writing a unit test that checks the desired output. Only after that do you implement the method. Finally, the unit test is used to verify whether the method's logic is correct or needs refining.

This reverse way of thinking made it much easier to code in a focused manner and spot potential problems early on. It was an "aha" moment to see how continuous testing during development.

Who should attend this workshop?

We recommend this workshop to developers looking to explore new approaches and improve their workflow. It is especially useful for ABAP developers with little to no experience in Test Driven Development (TDD), offering a hands-on opportunity to practice new methodologies that can be easily applied to everyday work.

Additionally, developers seeking to broaden their network and exchange ideas with colleagues from other companies will find this event beneficial. The collaboration during Pair Programming and the sharing of ideas provides a chance to view problems from multiple perspectives.

The ABAP Code Retreat was a valuable experience for us, helping us grow both professionally and personally. We’re looking forward to future events where we can further deepen our knowledge and make new connections.

~Ben

~@l_e_n_a 

~@Hoolya

entry.title: Re: Dump Created while fetching data TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED
entry.content:
Hello @AadivaramAbbulu 

You can split your processing into chunks using OPEN CURSOR with PACKAGE SIZE n addition and FETCH statements. Use joint select from CDHDR and CDPOS to avoid FOR ALL ENTRIES, that might also cause issues when resulting OR condition is too large.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard and Universal Model Integration with SAP BW via OData Service
entry.content:
Our application can be integrated to On-premise systems such as SAP BW via Smart Data Integration (SDI) through HANA Adapter. You can see the series of steps on how it can be done here on this blog post SAP PaPM Cloud Integration with SAP BW.

Both SAP PaPM Cloud SM and UM supports OdataV4. In addition, SAP PaPM Cloud UM is designed also to support ODataV2. Therefore, on this blog post, I will show you how both SAP PaPM Cloud SM and UM can be integrated with On-premise (BW) and consume data records using OData Service with Cloud Connector.

For you to have preview how the Integration Architecture looks like, here’s a simple visualization:

ODataV4 and ODataV2 has a different step-by-step procedure on how it is being created on the BW System. Let’s start first on how we can create the service in BW for ODataV4.

Creation of an OdataV4 service in SEGW:

With NW750, in SEGW, ODataV4 project can be created. Though it is not recommended as described on SAP Note SAP Note 2485370, project creation will still go through, and users can generate runtime objects that can be use on the service that will be created.

Once the Project is created. Users can import the DDIC structure to the Data Model. In this example, I will be using the DDIC Table generated from an existing ADSO

  1. On the created Project in SEGW, press right click to Data Model
  2. Choose Import
  3. Then choose DDIC Structure
  4. Add Entity name
  5. Search the existing Table generated from ADSO
  6. Choose Inbound Table generated from ADSO
  7. Next

Once the DDIC table was chosen, users may now proceed on fields mapping that will be required for the scenario.

  1. Choose the required fields
  2. Next
  3. Ensure that you have Key field
  4. Finish

On this Import example above, I have imported an existing ADSO using BW Fields rather than BW InfoObject. as the system does not allow fields that contains “/BIC” as its technical field name for ODataV4 project type in SEGW.

For more information, please refer to the official documentation Importing a DDIC Structure.

Once everything is mapped, users can generate the runtime objects which are Service ID, Classes that will be needed for the service creation.

With the created OdataV4 Project. We can now move to Registration and Publishing of the Service.

A.      Registration of the ODataV4 Service

1. Go to SAP back-end Service Administration via tcode /iwbep/v4_admin. Then Register Group

2. Fill up the necessary details to create the Service Group.

3. After filling up the creation of Service group, the system will ask if you would like to register an existing service. Fill up the information with the Generated Runtime objects details from previous steps.

Note: We have used the Extension of Model and Provider as changes are still needed for the generated runtime objects. Since PaPM Cloud SM Model View Odata only supports OdataV4, users have to perform a Code based implementation. 

SAMPLE CODE:

METHOD /iwbep/if_v4_dp_basic~read_entity_list.
    DATA: lv_entity_type_name TYPE /iwbep/if_v4_med_element=>ty_e_med_internal_name,
          ls_done_list        TYPE /iwbep/if_v4_requ_basic_list=>ty_s_todo_process_list,
          ls_todo_list        TYPE /iwbep/if_v4_requ_basic_list=>ty_s_todo_list,
          lt_data             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF /BIC/AZQAM_DEMO2.
    io_request->get_entity_type( IMPORTING ev_entity_type_name = lv_entity_type_name ).
    io_request->get_todos( IMPORTING es_todo_list = ls_todo_list ).

    CASE lv_entity_type_name.
      WHEN 'DEMO_ENTITY'.
        SELECT * FROM /BIC/AZQAM_DEMO2
         INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @lt_data.

        IF ls_todo_list-return-count = abap_true.
          io_response->set_count( lines( lt_data ) ).
        ENDIF.

        io_response->set_busi_data( it_busi_data = lt_data ).
        io_response->set_is_done( ls_done_list ).
      WHEN OTHERS.
        super->/iwbep/if_v4_dp_basic~read_entity_list( io_request  = io_request
                                                       io_response = io_response ).
    ENDCASE.
  ENDMETHOD.

Disclaimer: For any issues with the Odata V4 code based implementation, please refer to blog post: OData V4 code based implementation - Overview.

4. Once done, we can proceed on assigning the service to the created Group.

B.  Publish the ODataV4 Service

1. Go to SAP Gateway Service Administration via tcode /iwfnd/v4_admin. Then choose Publish Service Groups.

2. Search and select the Service Group created from previous steps. Then choose, Publish Service Groups

3. Once the service is published, go to transaction code /iwbep/v4_admin. Choose the assigned service created from previous steps and do Service Test to Gateway Client like the image below.

4. Choose the Entity set from the project created from SEGW. With this, it will generate the request URL .

 5. Execute the request URL to show the data records available in OdataV4 format.

With these results, we can now proceed on the next steps to create a connection between our BW and SAP PaPM Cloud SM / UM. But before that, let me show you first how we can create the ODataV2 service in BW.

Creation of an OdataV2 service in SEGW:

With the current version of NW750, in SEGW transaction code, OdataV2 service can be generated.

For the step-by-step procedure, you may refer to Creating a Service Builder Project.

Once the Project is created. Users can import the DDIC structure to the Data Model. It could be your ADSO, Table or Views.

For more information, please refer to the official documentation Importing a DDIC Structure.

If mapping of all fields and setting a primary are done, users may now proceed on mapping the Service Implementation to the Data Source.

  1. Under the project created, expand the folder Service Implementation.
  2. Right Click to the Entity Set generated from Import.
  3. Choose Map data to source.

After the previous Step C, users need to maintain the Data Source Attributes.

  1. Choose the Type ‘Business Entity’
  2. Go to Help button
  3. Choose SADL Model Type ‘DDIC Dictionary’ (This one is used for our example)
  4. Choose the Model that was imported from previous step
  5. Press Continue
  6. Press again Continue to finalize the mapping to data source

Upon pressing the continue button from Step F. The UI will look like the image below. To finalize the mapping, users need to click the Generate Mapping button on top of the table of the mapped fields.

Note: Unlike in ODataV4 project type. ODataV2 in SEGW accepts BW ADSO that contains InfoObject fields with “/BIC” as its mapped technical name like the image below.

Once all the fields are mapped, users now need to Generate the Runtime Objects.

  1. Press Generate Runtime Objects.
  2. All values on the pop-up windows are system generated, users may proceed to press Continue button.
  3. Add $TMP on package.
  4. Press Save button

When it is saved, it will provide a message log below that the Runtime Objects for the Project created were generated successfully.

A.      Publishing of the ODataV2 Service

The Runtime Objects and Service were successfully generated. But this still needs to be published for the user to make it appear on the Active services in our BW system.

  1. Go to transaction code: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.
  2. Choose Add Service button.

      3. Search for the Technical Service name using the Service generated from pressing Generate Runtime Objects from previous step.

  1. Select the searched Service.
  2. Choose Add Selected Service
  3. Add $TMP
  4. Press Continue

B.      Calling the ODataV2 Service

Since the service is already created, registered, and published, users may now proceed to call the ODataV2 service.

  1. Go back to transaction code: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and filter outby searching the service created.
  2. Calling the service in the Gateway Client
  1. Double click the published service.
  2. Under ICF Nodes section, choose Gateway Client 

     3. In the Gateway Client, press Execute button to verify if the service is successfully implemented. It will return status_code 200 means that the service can be called successfully.
     4. Let’s call now the Entity set that was imported from previous step.

  1. On the header, press EntitySets.
  2. Choose the EntitySet created from previous steps.
  3. Press Execute button.
  4. See all available data records in the HTTP Response in XML format.

With the successful execution, this service path can now be called in our SAP PaPM Cloud UM as this is the model that supports ODataV2.

Let’s proceed now on the creation of Connection between your BW and SAP PaPM Cloud SM or UM.

Creation of Connection in Cloud Connector

A.      Registering your SAP PaPM Cloud SM Instance or Tenant

On registration of your tenant in the Cloud Connector, please refer to the blog post Integration: Setup SAP PaPM Cloud to use RFA FI-GL and FI-GLI to post to your ECC under the section of A. in Cloud Connector: Registering your SAP PaPM Cloud Instance or Tenant.

B. In Cloud Connector: Register your internal System in the created Sub-account

  1. Choose the Subaccount that needs to be connected to the On-premise system
  2. Choose Cloud to On-Premise from the left menu pane
  3. Follow the steps that are being shown in the GIF below:

C.      In Cloud Connector: Register the resources of the added Internal System

The resources to be registered is the URL Path /sap. This is for the connection to know where the request will direct.

SAP BTP: Double check the Cloud Connector

If Sections A to C were completed, you should see the same reflecting in BTP > Cloud Connectors as Connected / Available

Connected
this means your Subaccount is connected in Cloud Connector

Available
this means your Internal system has resources added path (like /sap)

SAP BTP: Create Destination

Now that everything is configured in Cloud connector, Destination Administrator may now proceed to create a new Destination (HTTP Destination)

  1. Under your subaccount, go to Destinations.
  2. Choose New Destination
  3. Fill the Destination Fields with information from your HTTP Connection
  4. Add the Client of the Internal system in the Properties
  5. Save the configuration.

SAP PaPM Cloud SM Modeling – OdataV4

1. Create the Connection according to your Destination and Service details.

2. In your Modeling screen, create now the Model View OData maintaining the connection created from previous step. 

 3. By choosing the connection, PaPM Cloud SM will be able to detect all available fields from the Odata service that Modeler can use in the Fields Mapping.

With the successful connection, activation and execution will return a successful log. Modeler can now validate the consumed data records in the Show screen and use it for Complex Calculations and Reporting in SAP PaPM Cloud SM.

As mentioned from the introduction of this blog post, ODataV2 is not supported in SAP PaPM Cloud SM as it only supports ODataV4 service. See documentation: Model View OData .

With this, let’s move to the next procedures of this blog post.

SAP PaPM Cloud UM Modeling - ODataV2

  1. Creation of Connection that will use the Destination created from previous step.
  1. Add the Connection ID.
  2. Put the description of the Connection.
  3. Use the destination created from previous step.
  4. Add the existing service path from generated service.
  5. Add Default Key Field.
  6. Choose Create

With the successful creation, the EntitySet created from previous step will be visible in the UI of SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model where the user can perform the field mapping.

For further information with the creation of Connection and mapping, please refer to Manage Connections: Odata Service.

2. Fetch the data records of the OdataV2 service using Reader function.

In SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model, to fetch the available data records in an Odata Service, users need to create a Reader function on top of the synced Model Entity from our connection.

Users may now execute the Reader function. Data records are available under the Show Data screen of the Reader and the Synced Model Entity from Connection. See steps from the image below:

Reader:

 Synced Model Entity:

Now, I have shown you how ODataV4 in SAP PaPM Cloud SM and ODataV2 in SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model are successfully consumed via connection. Since Universal Model also supports ODataV4. Let me show it to you as well.

Technically, the steps are the same, users just must change the service path that is being used for ODataV4. Let’s proceed on the next step below

SAP PaPM Cloud UM Modeling – OdataV4

The service root used from the creation of Connection in SAP PaPM Cloud SM can be used as well in the creation of connection in SAP PaPM Cloud UM since we are going to consume the same ODataV4 service.

  1. Creation of Connection that will use the Destination created from previous step.
  1. Add the Connection ID.
  2. Put the description of the Connection.
  3. Use the destination created from previous step.
  4. Add the existing service path from generated service. (Same from PaPM Cloud SM)
  5. Add Default Key Field.
  6. Choose Create

Once the connection is created, it will show the available Entity set we have from the BW system then users may now perform the mapping of relevant fields for the scenario.

For further information with the creation of Connection and mapping, please refer to Manage Connections: Odata Service.

2. Fetch the data records of the OdataV4 service using Reader function.

Similar from ODataV2, in OdataV4, Reader Function should be RUN as well to fetch the data records to our Synced Model Entity and to the Reader function. Follow the steps in the image below for reference.

Reader:

 Model Entity:

Thank you so much for reaching here until the last step of our integration topic! After reading all through the steps of this blog post, we can conclude that in Integration between BW and SAP PaPM Cloud via OData service is possible with scenarios listed below:

  • SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model – it only supports ODataV4 service.
  • SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model – it supports ODataV2 and ODataV4 services.

I hope now you have learned the other way on how to integrate BW to our SAP PaPM Cloud Application.

See you again soon! On to the next integration topic 😊.

entry.title: Re: Migration of EHANA to Datasphere by leveraging Native HANA Cloud Instance of Datasphere Tenant
entry.content: Thank you @Jinal_Patel for the blog on migrating EHANA to Datasphere using a native HANA Cloud instance. Adding to @Vivek-RR's suggestions, while the migration strategy outlined presents a compelling approach, it's essential to consider certain restrictions associated with the HANA Cloud tenant of Datasphere. Let me add some comments from the Multi-Model & ML aspect of SAP HANA Cloud. According to SAP Notes 3463689, 3349720, and 2994416, it is important to highlight that directly leveraging advanced analytics capabilities like the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), Automated Predictive Library (APL), JSON Document Store or the Vector Engine (multi-model engines, also involves Graph Engine) can be challenging within this environment. These features are crucial for complex machine learning and AI-driven use cases, such as predictive modeling and vector-based similarity searches. However, due to certain limitations in Datasphere’s HANA Cloud tenant - like restricted access to administrative tools, custom configuration options, or the inability to fully utilize all the native functionalities of a stand alone HANA Cloud database - implementing these advanced analytics capabilities becomes less straightforward. These points are crucial for anyone planning a migration to be fully aware of and to assess how these restrictions might affect their specific use cases and system requirements. For further clarification on an effective migration strategy, I will defer it to the migration experts on my HANA Cloud team - samarjit.saha@sap.com

entry.title: Change format
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently one of our custom program is writing the Sales data as XML format in the App server by using OPEN DATASET/TRANSFER syntax the file is .dat format

Example file name: weekly.sales.may.dat     (Interfaces\OUT\weekly.sales.may.dat)

In AL11 transaction when I double click this file the example data is as below,

 

 

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<Batch>

<Record><OCR>0200</OCR><TXTMT><![CDATA[0200  Metals/Condensa]]></TXTMT><YEAR>2013</YEAR><BUKRS>1000</BUKRS><COST_CENTER>034181129V</COST_CENTER><ACCOUNT>0062500377</ACCOUNT><FAREA>A310</FAREA><MATKL><![CDATA[XX]]></MATKL><TSL>5502.10</TSL><MSL>59.270</MSL><UNIT>KG</UNIT><REGIO>CA</REGIO><KOSAR>L</KOSAR><ABTEI><![CDATA[31544]]></ABTEI><FCNTRFTUR><![CDATA[HARDITM4735702]]></FCNTRFTUR><DESCRIPTION><![CDATA[(BLK)A SEELIGSON]]></DESCRIPTION><RABUNODE></RABUNODE></Record>

<Record><OCR>0200</OCR><TXTMT><![CDATA[0200  Metals/Condensa]]></TXTMT><YEAR>2013</YEAR><BUKRS>1000</BUKRS><COST_CENTER>034181129V</COST_CENTER><ACCOUNT>0062500377</ACCOUNT><FAREA>A310</FAREA><MATKL><![CDATA[XX]]></MATKL><TSL>5502.10</TSL><MSL>59.270</MSL><UNIT>KG</UNIT><REGIO>CA</REGIO><KOSAR>L</KOSAR><ABTEI><![CDATA[31544]]></ABTEI><FCNTRFTUR><![CDATA[HARDITM4735702]]></FCNTRFTUR><DESCRIPTION><![CDATA[(BLK)A SEELIGSON]]></DESCRIPTION><RABUNODE></RABUNODE></Record>

<Record><OCR>0200</OCR><TXTMT><![CDATA[0200  Metals/Condensa]]></TXTMT><YEAR>2013</YEAR><BUKRS>1000</BUKRS><COST_CENTER>034181129V</COST_CENTER><ACCOUNT>0062500377</ACCOUNT><FAREA>A310</FAREA><MATKL><![CDATA[XX]]></MATKL><TSL>5502.10</TSL><MSL>59.270</MSL><UNIT>KG</UNIT><REGIO>CA</REGIO><KOSAR>L</KOSAR><ABTEI><![CDATA[31544]]></ABTEI><FCNTRFTUR><![CDATA[HARDITM4735702]]></FCNTRFTUR><DESCRIPTION><![CDATA[(BLK)A SEELIGSON]]></DESCRIPTION><RABUNODE></RABUNODE></Record>

</Batch>

entry.title: Re: Usage Decision Status Reversal for Non - stock relevant inspection.
entry.content:
THANKS! We used this and it works perfectly. Once the UD has been reversed (it is initialized) and saved, the inspection results for the lot are re-opened and can be adjusted. If this is a lot for an inspection point, new inspection points can be created. A new UD for the lot can be entered at any point after this, and if you save the UD and then realize (again) you made another mistake - well, you can reverse/initialize it again as many times as are required. ALSO - since it is a very big deal to reverse a UD, we created an authorization object for this function so we could control its distribution in the user community - allowing access only to limited supervisory personnel.  

NOTE - we did add a more descriptive label on the button.

NOTE ALSO - we set up similar functionality for stock-relevant inspection lots (in a separate app) based on this blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/reversal-of-usage-decision-and-stock-posting-of-inspection-lot/ba-p/13260588

entry.title: Re: Issue with Data Types: Batch Load Data into a Custom...
entry.content:
Thank you for your  question, @shashankagg.

I found this community blog which has some explicit code on uploading numeric data from spreadsheet, namely the part "for_numeric_value" in this line: from_row( xco_cp_xlsx=>coordinate->for_numeric_value( 2 ).

There might be other approaches: If the number you are attempting to import should have a dimension, ensure to also provide for that dimension. Decimals, if there are any, should be matching the target field's definition for decimals in every row. If the number is not treated like a number - e.g. because it is not used to calculate with, like a phone number, why not importing it as text/characters? Also, ensure the spreadsheet does not contain formula. Make it as plain as possible. 

if that helped solve your request, please mark it accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: How to create a CDS model/entity for quartz tables?
entry.content:
We are using a quartz scheduler with SAP Hana, it has some tables, and we wanted to get those tables created through CDS. I am trying to convert the table SQL to a CDS model/entity. I was able to create the tables but the constraints are missing. Quartz uses the composite primary key and it is referred to as a foreign key to other tables. Below are the SQL for tables :

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_JOB_DETAILS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    JOB_NAME  VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    JOB_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    DESCRIPTION VARCHAR(250) NULL,
    JOB_CLASS_NAME   VARCHAR(250) NOT NULL,
    IS_DURABLE VARCHAR(1) NOT NULL,
    IS_NONCONCURRENT VARCHAR(1) NOT NULL,
    IS_UPDATE_DATA VARCHAR(1) NOT NULL,
    REQUESTS_RECOVERY VARCHAR(1) NOT NULL,
    JOB_DATA BLOB NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,JOB_NAME,JOB_GROUP)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_TRIGGERS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    JOB_NAME  VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    JOB_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    DESCRIPTION VARCHAR(250) NULL,
    NEXT_FIRE_TIME BIGINT NULL,
    PREV_FIRE_TIME BIGINT NULL,
    PRIORITY INTEGER NULL,
    TRIGGER_STATE VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_TYPE VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL,
    START_TIME BIGINT NOT NULL,
    END_TIME BIGINT NULL,
    CALENDAR_NAME VARCHAR(200) NULL,
    MISFIRE_INSTR SMALLINT NULL,
    JOB_DATA BLOB NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP),
    FOREIGN KEY (SCHED_NAME,JOB_NAME,JOB_GROUP)
        REFERENCES QRTZ_JOB_DETAILS(SCHED_NAME,JOB_NAME,JOB_GROUP)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_SIMPLE_TRIGGERS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    REPEAT_COUNT BIGINT NOT NULL,
    REPEAT_INTERVAL BIGINT NOT NULL,
    TIMES_TRIGGERED BIGINT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP),
    FOREIGN KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
        REFERENCES QRTZ_TRIGGERS(SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_CRON_TRIGGERS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    CRON_EXPRESSION VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    TIME_ZONE_ID VARCHAR(80),
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP),
    FOREIGN KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
        REFERENCES QRTZ_TRIGGERS(SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_SIMPROP_TRIGGERS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    STR_PROP_1 VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    STR_PROP_2 VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    STR_PROP_3 VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    INT_PROP_1 INT NULL,
    INT_PROP_2 INT NULL,
    LONG_PROP_1 BIGINT NULL,
    LONG_PROP_2 BIGINT NULL,
    DEC_PROP_1 NUMERIC(13,4) NULL,
    DEC_PROP_2 NUMERIC(13,4) NULL,
    BOOL_PROP_1 VARCHAR(1) NULL,
    BOOL_PROP_2 VARCHAR(1) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP),
    FOREIGN KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
    REFERENCES QRTZ_TRIGGERS(SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_BLOB_TRIGGERS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    BLOB_DATA BLOB NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP),
    FOREIGN KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
        REFERENCES QRTZ_TRIGGERS(SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_CALENDARS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    CALENDAR_NAME  VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    CALENDAR BLOB NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,CALENDAR_NAME)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_PAUSED_TRIGGER_GRPS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_GROUP  VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,TRIGGER_GROUP)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_FIRED_TRIGGERS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    ENTRY_ID VARCHAR(95) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    TRIGGER_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    INSTANCE_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    FIRED_TIME BIGINT NOT NULL,
    SCHED_TIME BIGINT NOT NULL,
    PRIORITY INTEGER NOT NULL,
    STATE VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,
    JOB_NAME VARCHAR(200) NULL,
    JOB_GROUP VARCHAR(200) NULL,
    IS_NONCONCURRENT VARCHAR(1) NULL,
    REQUESTS_RECOVERY VARCHAR(1) NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,ENTRY_ID)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_SCHEDULER_STATE
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    INSTANCE_NAME VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    LAST_CHECKIN_TIME BIGINT NOT NULL,
    CHECKIN_INTERVAL BIGINT NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,INSTANCE_NAME)
);

CREATE TABLE QRTZ_LOCKS
  (
    SCHED_NAME VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
    LOCK_NAME  VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (SCHED_NAME,LOCK_NAME)
);

entry.title: Business Partners in CPQ – BRIM S/4 Solution Quotation Integration
entry.content:
Master Data is the engine of any integration scenario, which ensures seamlessly integration flow and data consistency. For the CPQ-BRIM integration scenario, Business Partners and Products play this  important role. In this scenario, CPQ is a S/4 HANA public cloud solution, where the Quote 2.0 engine integrates with S/4 HANA Private Cloud Billing and Revenue integration to help customers efficiently configure products for the customers, negotiate right prices and create right quotes to efficiently manage their sales process.

It is important to understand the key related terms viz – Business Partners, Partner functions and Involved Parties here.

  • Business partners of an organization are all the entities that have business relations with that organization.
  • Partner functions determine how a certain business partner is related to a transaction. The most common partner functions are Sold-To Party (entity to which goods are being sold), Ship-To Party (entity to which goods are being shipped), Payer (entity responsible for payment), Bill-To Party (entity to which invoice is issued), and so on.
  • The Involved parties tab in the quote is where users choose partner function and choose or add business partners to create an involved party. An involved party on a quote is a business partner matched with the partner function they perform on the quote. The prerequisite for managing involved parties on the quote is to create the Involved parties tab following this procedure. The tab is empty if there are no partner functions selected to be added by default on the quote.

Example

Lisa Adams is the buyer of the goods sold through a quote. Keshav Madhavan is replicated as a Business Partner from S/4 HANA into SAP CPQ, while the partner function Sold-to party is already in the CPQ system, created by default when the Business Partner feature is enabled. On the quote, the user creates a new involved party by selecting Keshav Madhavan from the list of existing business partners and Sold-to party from the list of partner functions.

 

The Business Partner data transitions from S/4 HANA to SAP Mater Data Integration (MDI) on BTP to SAP Cloud Integration and finally is replicated to SAP CPQ.

 

  1. Leading system for Business Partner creation in this integration is S/4 HANA, where integration is enabled with SAP Cloud Integration via SAP Mater Data Integration (MDI).
  2. SAP CPQ provide api: /api/businesspartner/v1/businesspartner for Business Partner CRUD operations.
  3. By default, tenants with the Business Partner feature use 3-letter country abbreviations.
  4. Type of Partner functions supported in this integration are:
    • Sold-To Party (mandatory) (SAP CPQ -> SetUp -> Workflow/Approval -> Required Fields by Workflow Action -> crossing of Sold-to party(Vertical) and Place Order(Horizontal)
    • Ship-To Party
    • Bill-To Party
    • Payer Party
    • Contact Person
    • Employee Responsible
  5. The SAP BRIM can only deal with one value per Partner Function. Therefore, the SAP CPQ system must not send more than one Business Partner for each Partner Function.
    • SetUp -> Quotes -> Partner Functions
  6. Partner Functions can only be replicated from SAP CPQ to SAP BRIM if the Partner Function Key exists in SAP BRIM and was entered on the More tab in the integration flow configuration.
    • Navigate to CPI Instance
    • Design – Package: SAP CPQ - Quote 2.0 Integration with SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management
    • Click on Artifact Replicate Quote 2.0 from SAP CPQ to SAP S4HANA
    • Click Configure
    • Click More
    • Sample screenshot below                

                 

7. Once the BP is created in S/4 it can be automatically replicated by using DRF or you can replicate it manually by using the DRF tile "Replicate by Object selection. The Business Partner Replication can either be triggered directly from SAP Billing Revenue and Innovation Management to SAP CPQ or via SAP Master Data Integration. Both scenarios can make use of the same iFlow in SAP Cloud Integration as the inbound Business Partner object has the same structure. However, the settings in SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management determine if the Business Partner is replicated directly from SAP S/4HANA to SAP CPQ or from SAP S/4HANA via SAP MDI to SAP CPQ.

8. In SAP CPQ, ID displayed in the field Partner ID on business partner should match the business partner in SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management.         

                   

                      

9. The quote created in CPQ must contain an involved party with this partner function and a business partner. The system then uses the assigned business partner to filter the contracts from SAP S/4HANA and display only the contracts related to that business partner when creating a new asset in the change process scenario.

entry.title: How to Understand the HANA Generated CalculationScenario
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have below design 

HANA CVs(Ex: CV1,CV2 ) With Input Parameters >> Used as Sources In HANA BW HCPR (EX:CP1) >> Generate External CV for HCPR CP1

The Generated CV ( "_SYS_BIC"."system-local.bw.bw2hana/CP1" with Input Params ) Internally use some generated object as Source which will be in this pattern :  "<Schema-Name>"."0BW:BIA:CP1"

Questions:
a) How can we under stand that Calculation Scenario Script , As its not SQL.

b) When I use this Generated CV ("system-local.bw.bw2hana/CP1") of CP1  as Source in any further CV , Will that Input Params gets pushed back to the Base CV's ( CV1,CV2 base nodes ) Via CalculationScenario Script ? 

 

Br

Kiran K

entry.title: Real Talk with Britta & Eduardo | Data & Analytics Track Leads
entry.content:
Hello everybody! I’m Eduardo, a Product Manager in Data & Analytics, with a focus on SAP  Data Intelligence and SAP Datasphere. I was born in Brazil and love travelling the world with my family, meet people, discover new cultures and different types of food.

Hi guys, I’m Britta, a Product Manager for Data & Analytics. I live in “Pfalz”, a nice region in Germany, close to Walldorf, where we enjoy delicious white wine. When I’m not dealing with SAP, I love playing the violin and hang out with my kids.

“Data is at the heart of the new, sustainable enterprise!”

Yes, indeed it is. Data & Analytics gives organizations a competitive advantage, enabling informed decisions and uncovering new opportunities. Especially when you think of how powerful AI has become, opportunities are infinite. However, the best AI models are only as strong as its database. Poor data leads to flawed predictions and underperforming AIs.

Our track provides a set of hot topics and new features along our closely integrated product portfolio to help you to stay in control of your data:

  1. SAP Datasphere serves as SAP’s Business Data Fabric solution: Deliver trusted, context-rich data to every user with business context​ and logic intact​.
  2. Build Intelligent Data Applications with SAP HANA​: Utilize generative AI, context-awareness,​ and connected to vital business data.
  3. Extended Planning and Analysis​ with SAP Analytics Cloud: Elevate enterprise planning with an optimized, vertically integrated consumption layer for planning and analytics workloads.
  4. Improve your business-critical information with Master Data Governance.

What inspired you to specialize in this track?

With this portfolio we empower organizations to make more informed, efficient, and strategic decisions. We are also very excited to collaborate with so many outstanding SAP experts and customers. This is what inspired us to specialize in this track.

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit?

In Data & Analytics, we turn abstract concepts and creative thoughts into tangible outcomes in our solutions. In Clean Core for example, SAP HANA’s integration with SAP Build allows you to easily create AI-driven, context-aware applications while keeping workloads separate from your ERP system. In our lecture DA102, Henkel will share how they optimized trade promotions with HANA Cloud ML capabilities.

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar):

Kicking your journey off we recommend watching the Track Intro “DA100 – Data in the Age of AI” with Tobias and Britta. We will summarize our complete lecture portfolio, talk about recent deliveries and investment areas for the future and introduce some deep-dive topics.

  1. DA103: Generative AI–driven business transformation enabled by SAP HANA Cloud: Hear from Markus and Christoph how SAP HANA supports AI initiatives.
  2. DA106: Integration of SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere solutions: Listen to Maximilian and see how we bring two solutions more and more together - #BetterTogether!
  3. DA110: How to treat data as a product: Join Suva and Florian to learn about how Data Products together with SAP Datasphere will foster agility.
  4. DA200: Best features of SAP BW in SAP Datasphere: Learn from Dominik and Klaus-Peter about features you know from SAP BW that have made their way natively into SAP Datasphere.

 Please do not forget to register for our two Jumpstart sessions DA180 and DA181.

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024?

  • Comfortable desk and chair: As TechEd is 100% virtual this year, it is important to feel comfortable - feel free to stay in your pajamas 😉!
  • Be open and curious to explore the sessions we have prepared for you!
  • Agenda Builder - as you will find many interesting sessions, this will help you maximize your time. But don’t worry, if you miss sessions, look for the recording afterwards.

Check out our Session Catalog and register for the lectures of your interest!

See you at Teched &  

Britta & Eduardo

entry.title: Re: Two-tier ERP and Group Reporting: Deployment Options with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for your suggestions.

You are right that GRDC apps and the required services are hosted on SAP Business Cloud Platform (BTP). GRDC integrates with S/4HANA for group reporting – cloud or OP – via APIs.

GRDC is part of SAP's product strategy for consolidation. If customers run SAP S/4HANA for group reporting in combination with SAP S/4HANA private cloud or S4HANA public cloud, the SAP Group Reporting Data Collection (GRDC) is a tool which enables connection of SAP or Non-SAP data from different sources to the Group Reporting in an automated way. It runs on the BTP environment. For more information, users can click here

However, it is technically feasible to upload data extracts from subsidiaries to Headquarters without GRDC in case the customer decides not to leverage GRDC for group reporting purposes.

In summary, diagrams 1 and 2 represent possible Two-tier ERP deployment options for Group Reporting.

Regarding Central Finance part, users can refer to SAP note - 3398470 - Central Finance: Restriction Note for the integration of an SAP S/4HANA Cloud source system to Central Finance

entry.title: Re: Send the PR data to HANA without Budget check
entry.content:
Please check

https://support.ariba.com/item/view/196910

https://support.ariba.com/item/view/176316

3239250 - How does the Application.Budget.CheckBudgetDuringFinalApproval parameter work?

Symptom

How does the Application.Budget.CheckBudgetDuringFinalApproval parameter work?

Resolution

The parameter Application.Budget.CheckBudgetDuringFinalApproval is used to check the budget when the final approver approves the purchase requisition (PR) document. This will only take effect if the final approver is a member of the Budget Approver group. The PR will go through without checking the budget if a normal user approves the requisition.

entry.title: Assembly Processing (PS)
entry.content:
Hello gurus,

I'm working with the assembly processing scenario, using quotation and network. I have two "materials," both linked to the same standard network. The standard network, in turn, is associated with a standard project that contains only one WBS element.

The process works perfectly for both materials (Screenshot_00.jpg and Screenshot_01.jpg), but only when the sales document (which is the quotation) has a single item.

 

 When I add a second item (Screenshot_02.jpg), an error occurs (Screenshot_03.jpg).

 

It seems like the WBS element to be created is validated in the sales document before it's actually generated, or the WBS element is simply not created at all.

I'm using version 2024. I believe this might be a program error, as I've already encountered another issue where I had to apply SAP note 3408903 because the system wasn't even creating the operative network.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or can offer any help? @SAPSupport 

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.content:
Hello @RumzaanSultan 

As per note 2115337 - BAPI or MASS Options for RFQs - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA there is no standard BAPI for requests for quotation. You'd need to use a batch input to ME41.

entry.title: SAP Lighthouse Program - Partner Use Cases adopting BTP GenAI Capabilities [Part 2]
entry.content:
The SAP office of the CTO sponsors the SAP BTP Lighthouse Program, which provides advanced access and hands-on support for BTP services with GenAI capabilities to partners and customers. Partners are crucial to SAP's goal to help the world run better and improve people’s lives, and the Lighthouse program's partner track helps them deliver GenAI use cases using SAP resources.

In part 2 of this 3-part series, I will highlight some of the amazing work that participating partners have created leveraging SAP Business AI services such as:

SAP Build Code: SAP’s Generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, optimized for Java and JavaScript application development.

Our partner, PwC leverages SAP Build Code to build a Speech-Enabled Auto-Commenting Assistant that helps companies access useful information from unstructured documents for multiple customers.

Wipro uses SAP Build Code to build a Smart Energy Procurement Advisor Tool for power (electric) procurement planners of Energy and Utility companies to have a clear plan for managing energy consumption demands by recommending optimally priced energy source mix and minimal disruption.

Check out part one of the series for partners that have adopted SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and SAP Generative AI Hub. You can find out more about these partners and SAP BTP GenAI services here. Looking for even more adoption stories - there is exciting news coming up from the Lighthouse program partners and our product teams at SAP TechEd, Oct 8-9 2024, Register Today!

entry.title: Standard iflow for SF-S4 Employee availability replication
entry.content:
I have the package "SAP SuccessFactors - Employee Availability Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud " implemented in production. I think SAP recommend to schedule this daily and it generally runs only once a day technically even if we schedule hourly.  How can we get it run near Realtime to get the Delta's run immediately? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Standard iflow for SF-S4 Employee availability replic...
entry.content:
This availability service has limitation that it can not replicate delta in real time. the iflow only replicates data once day, however, it can triggered on ADHOC basis to replicate the data any time by setting the parameter "Test" mode to "True".

Generally, availability data does not change every hour and that is why delta is not required more than once a day.

Anyone had such a scenario before? Thanks!

entry.title: S/4HANA for product Compliance NEW Dangerous Goods Management - unable to trigger classification
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I am trying to set up the Product Compliance on S/4HANA, and go through the end-to-end process of completing the assessment - specifically for Dangerous Goods Management solution on S4HANA 2023.

Unfortunately, I am unable to trigger the assessment - I am expecting the item to appear in my worklist in the "my unpackaged dangerous goods" app, after assigning the compliance purpose to the CCI. I am following the best practice steps in accordance to 3FC in-scope item, and have performed the following: 

  1. created semi-finished material - set to compliance relevant
  2. created finished material - set to compliance relevant
  3. created compliance purpose, and assigned a Compliance Requirement available from the embedded content (IMDG-CODE)
  4. created unpackaged product. assigned the compliance purpose, related materials, related packaged products, created substances/compositions

According to the 3FC test script, the next step is to classify the product. However, the unpackaged product does not appear in the "my unpackaged dangerous goods" app. I am unsure if this is a bug or missing configuration or missing data at this point. 

To note, this issue also occurs in the other solution areas such as Product Marketability. 

Please refer to the attached screenshots where you can see that the compliance purpose has been assigned, yet it does not show in the worklist.
